mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5 percent, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization improvements and supply chain stabilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following an INDPRO increase, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 215.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and trigger eventual monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the predictive slope turns positive in walk-forward validation tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad economic expansion that directly lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDP growth, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, manufacturing capacity utilization drops sharply and supply chain bottlenecks resolve into demand destruction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of months where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward falsification tests.
